evidence_refs.source_quote: Community Board 5 Transportation & Environment Committee, May 19, 2025
In this meeting, we are presenting a draft proposal

payload.description: Implementation of 34th Street enhanced bus priority measures

evidence_refs.source_quote: Implementation in Summer/Fall 2025

payload.claim_text: The 14th Street Busway pilot launched in October 2019, in conjunction with M14A/D Select Bus Service. Bus speeds increased up to 24%. Crashes along the corridor decreased by 42%. Travel speeds for streets around the busway had minimal changes. In post-implementation surveys, participants mentioned faster, more frequent bus service and people feel safer crossing the street.
payload.subject: 14th Street Busway

evidence_refs.source_quote: Pilot launched in October 2019, in conjunction with M14A/D Select Bus Service • Bus speeds increased up to 24% • Crashes along the corridor decreased by 42% • Travel speeds for streets around the busway had minimal changes

payload.claim_text: ACE camera enforcement is successful in changing driver behavior. The number of violations decreases over time as drivers adjust to busway violations. ~80% of violators don't get a second ticket. Human review by DOT process ensures violations are captured and issued according to program rules.
payload.subject: ACE camera enforcement on 34th Street

evidence_refs.source_quote: Data shows that camera enforcement is successful in changing driver behavior – The number of violations decreases over time as drivers adjust to busway violations – ~80% of violators don't get a second ticket

payload.claim_text: DOT on-street engagement found: pedestrians concerned about overcrowding on sidewalks, long crossing distances, and vehicular congestion; bus riders noted slow bus speeds and long wait times; businesses noted need for additional loading space. Community Board requested 24/7 14th Street style busway, sidewalk widening, pedestrian safety elements, and design for deliveries.
payload.subject: Community feedback on 34th Street

evidence_refs.source_quote: DOT on-street engagement: – Overcrowding on sidewalks, long crossing distances, and vehicular congestion were primary concerns for pedestrians – Bus riders noted slow bus speeds and long wait times – Businesses noted a need for additional loading space on and around the corridor • Community Board Feedback: – Request for a 24/7 14 th Street style busway – Request for sidewalk widening, and other pedestrian safety elements – Request for design and regulations to facilitate deliveries
